reasoning:
Looking at "theca" - a biological protective sheath that forms organically around something valuable, adapting its thickness and permeability based on conditions. This suggests a retirement mechanism that encases value adaptively rather than demanding fixed deposits.

The core problem with variable income isn't willpower or access - it's that every dollar *feels* essential when you can't predict the next one. Traditional systems ask "what can you spare?" which paralyzes irregular earners.

Let me invert this entirely:

```json
{
  "label": "Natal Security Debt with Adaptive Servicing",
  "design_principles": "Invert the retirement model: don't save forward, repay backward. Everyone starts with a debt-backed security endowment. Your earnings service it; variability becomes irrelevant because the rate adapts. Death forgives.",
  "core_mechanism": "At birth, you receive access to a protected security account (~$400K) as DEBT, not savings. All income automatically services this debt at algorithmically-determined variable rates based on real-time capacity.",
  "how_it_works": "Banking integration detects income events in real-time. Each deposit triggers micro-calculation: (income - essential expenses - volatility buffer) × age-adjusted coefficient = repayment amount. High months pay 15-25%; zero-income months pay nothing and allow emergency draws. The theca 'thickens' (debt reduces) during abundance and remains protective during scarcity. Reaching ~60, remaining debt converts to pension entitlement or forgiveness based on work-years. Drawing on the account during working years is possible but extends the repayment horizon. Research needed: optimal coefficients, essential expense detection, abuse prevention.",
  "what_is_new": "Eliminates the 'save for retirement' framing entirely. You're born with security; work is repayment, not accumulation. Variable income naturally handled because the debt doesn't demand consistency - it adapts.",
  "why_it_works": "Variable earners' core problem is psychological: every dollar feels necessary. By making retirement pre-funded (as debt), the decision moves from 'can I afford to save?' to 'how much can I pay down?' Debt repayment is psychologically easier than savings accumulation. The protective 'theca' exists from birth - you're never unprotected, never behind.",
  "why_it_fails": "Creates massive initial liability requiring either intergenerational transfer or money creation. Moral hazard: those who draw heavily and die early 'win.' Political framing of 'debt from birth' may horrify people. Gaming through expense inflation. Requires unprecedented financial surveillance to calculate repayment rates accurately.",
  "medium_term": "Gig economy workers stop choosing between rent and retirement. Emergency draws replace predatory lending. 'Retirement anxiety' becomes 'debt progress tracking' - psychologically manageable. Early adopter countries see labor mobility increase.",
  "long_term_vision": "The concept of 'retirement preparedness' disappears as a category. Everyone is born with a security theca that they spend their working life reinforcing. Income volatility becomes irrelevant to life security. Work becomes about meaning and debt reduction rather than survival accumulation. The framing shift - from building future security to servicing existing protection - fundamentally changes the psychology of economic precarity."
}
```

solution.label: Natal Security Debt with Adaptive Servicing
solution.design_principles: Invert the retirement model: don't save forward, repay backward. Everyone starts with a debt-backed security endowment. Your earnings service it; variability becomes irrelevant because the rate adapts. Death forgives.
solution.core_mechanism: At birth, you receive access to a protected security account (~$400K) as DEBT, not savings. All income automatically services this debt at algorithmically-determined variable rates based on real-time capacity.
solution.how_it_works: Banking integration detects income events in real-time. Each deposit triggers micro-calculation: (income - essential expenses - volatility buffer) × age-adjusted coefficient = repayment amount. High months pay 15-25%; zero-income months pay nothing and allow emergency draws. The theca 'thickens' (debt reduces) during abundance and remains protective during scarcity. Reaching ~60, remaining debt converts to pension entitlement or forgiveness based on work-years. Drawing on the account during working years is possible but extends the repayment horizon. Research needed: optimal coefficients, essential expense detection, abuse prevention.
solution.what_is_new: Eliminates the 'save for retirement' framing entirely. You're born with security; work is repayment, not accumulation. Variable income naturally handled because the debt doesn't demand consistency - it adapts.
solution.why_it_works: Variable earners' core problem is psychological: every dollar feels necessary. By making retirement pre-funded (as debt), the decision moves from 'can I afford to save?' to 'how much can I pay down?' Debt repayment is psychologically easier than savings accumulation. The protective 'theca' exists from birth - you're never unprotected, never behind.
solution.why_it_fails: Creates massive initial liability requiring either intergenerational transfer or money creation. Moral hazard: those who draw heavily and die early 'win.' Political framing of 'debt from birth' may horrify people. Gaming through expense inflation. Requires unprecedented financial surveillance to calculate repayment rates accurately.
solution.medium_term: Gig economy workers stop choosing between rent and retirement. Emergency draws replace predatory lending. 'Retirement anxiety' becomes 'debt progress tracking' - psychologically manageable. Early adopter countries see labor mobility increase.
solution.long_term_vision: The concept of 'retirement preparedness' disappears as a category. Everyone is born with a security theca that they spend their working life reinforcing. Income volatility becomes irrelevant to life security. Work becomes about meaning and debt reduction rather than survival accumulation. The framing shift - from building future security to servicing existing protection - fundamentally changes the psychology of economic precarity.
